body.content:
Kyoto Three-Day Itinerary - Inside Kyoto
Three days in Kyoto allows you to explore the main sightseeing districts and then head off the beaten path into the northern mountains. This is the perfect way to spend three full days in Kyoto.
![Sun-dappled path through the Sagano Bamboo forest, Arashiyama, Kyoto](https://photos.smugmug.com/photos/i-vCMn3cm/0/45d6eff3/L/i-vCMn3cm-L.jpg "Sun-dappled path through the Sagano Bamboo forest, Arashiyama, Kyoto")Sagano Bamboo forest, Arashiyama, Kyoto © [caseyyee](https://www.flickr.com/photos/caseyyee/4278141407)
### First: A Few Things to Keep in Mind
The first two days of this itinerary are the same as our [Kyoto Two-Day Itinerary](https://www.insidekyoto.com/kyoto-two-day-itinerary). These two days cover Kyoto’s most popular sightseeing districts: [Southern Higashiyama](https://www.insidekyoto.com/southern-higashiyama) and [Arashiyama](https://www.insidekyoto.com/arashiyama) (the third day covers less popular areas). Thus, this is a good itinerary to do in the off-season (summer or winter). In high-season (spring and fall), it may be too crowded for comfort. If you will be in Kyoto in high-season, or simply like quieter spots, check out our [Off the Beaten Track Itineraries](https://www.insidekyoto.com/kyoto-off-the-beaten-track-itineraries). If you’ll be in town during cherry blossom season, check out our [Kyoto Cherry Blossom Itinerary](https://www.insidekyoto.com/kyoto-cherry-blossom-itinerary).
Top Activities In Kyoto
[![A woman in Japanese kimono and makeup poses with a parasol in a traditional Kyoto street, looking directly into the camera](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20width='auto'%20height='auto'%20viewBox='0%200%20auto%20auto'%3E%3C/svg%3E "A woman in Japanese kimono and makeup poses with a parasol in a traditional Kyoto street, looking directly into the camera")](https://www.insidekyoto.com/kimono-rental-in-kyoto?utm_source=grid&utm_medium=link&utm_id=IK-Activities-Grid-01)[Kimono Rental
In Kyoto](https://www.insidekyoto.com/kimono-rental-in-kyoto?utm_source=grid&utm_medium=link&utm_id=IK-Activities-Grid-02)Rent a kimono and
hire a photographer
for the afternoon[More Info](https://www.insidekyoto.com/kimono-rental-in-kyoto?utm_source=grid&utm_medium=link&utm_id=IK-Activities-Grid-03)
[![Head priest Miyazaki-san, wearing robes, talks with a visitor in the intricately-decorated main hall of the zen Kosei-ji Temple in Kyoto, Japan](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20width='auto'%20height='auto'%20viewBox='0%200%20auto%20auto'%3E%3C/svg%3E "Head priest Miyazaki-san, wearing robes, talks with a visitor in the intricately-decorated main hall of the zen Kosei-ji Temple in Kyoto, Japan")](https://www.insidekyoto.com/zen-meditation-at-kosei-ji-temple-in-kyoto?utm_source=grid&utm_medium=link&utm_id=IK-Activities-Grid-01)[Kosei-ji Temple
Zen Meditation](https://www.insidekyoto.com/zen-meditation-at-kosei-ji-temple-in-kyoto?utm_source=grid&utm_medium=link&utm_id=IK-Activities-Grid-02)Experience zazen
(seated Zen meditation)
at a Kyoto temple[More Info](https://www.insidekyoto.com/zen-meditation-at-kosei-ji-temple-in-kyoto?utm_source=grid&utm_medium=link&utm_id=IK-Activities-Grid-03)
[![A kneeling woman in traditional Japanese outfit raises a cup during a Kyoto tea ceremony](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20width='auto'%20height='auto'%20viewBox='0%200%20auto%20auto'%3E%3C/svg%3E "A kneeling woman in traditional Japanese outfit raises a cup during a Kyoto tea ceremony")](https://www.insidekyoto.com/kyoto-tea-ceremony?utm_source=grid&utm_medium=link&utm_id=IK-Activities-Grid-01)[Kyoto Tea
Ceremony](https://www.insidekyoto.com/kyoto-tea-ceremony?utm_source=grid&utm_medium=link&utm_id=IK-Activities-Grid-02)The home of the tea
ceremony remains the
best place to try it[More Info](https://www.insidekyoto.com/kyoto-tea-ceremony?utm_source=grid&utm_medium=link&utm_id=IK-Activities-Grid-03)
[![Red torils donated by Kansai Television flank the pathway through Fushimi-Inari Taisha Shrine in Kyoto, Japan](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20width='auto'%20height='auto'%20viewBox='0%200%20auto%20auto'%3E%3C/svg%3E "Red torils donated by Kansai Television flank the pathway through Fushimi-Inari Taisha Shrine in Kyoto, Japan")](https://www.insidekyoto.com/fushimi-inari-hike-kyoto?utm_source=grid&utm_medium=link&utm_id=IK-Activities-Grid-01)[Fushimi-Inari
Hike](https://www.insidekyoto.com/fushimi-inari-hike-kyoto?utm_source=grid&utm_medium=link&utm_id=IK-Activities-Grid-02)Hike to the summit
of Mount Inari-san and
visit the shrines[More Info](https://www.insidekyoto.com/fushimi-inari-hike-kyoto?utm_source=grid&utm_medium=link&utm_id=IK-Activities-Grid-03)
##### Hire A Travel Expert To Plan Your Japan Itinerary
If you would like to hire a travel expert to help you plan your own customised Japan itinerary, [please contact my company Japan Travel Consulting](https://www.insidekyoto.com/japan-travel-consulting) to discuss your needs.
### Itinerary Summary
* **Duration:** Three days of 6 to 8 hours of touring (not including evening strolls).
* **Areas Covered:** [Southern Higashiyama](https://www.insidekyoto.com/southern-higashiyama), [Downtown](https://www.insidekyoto.com/downtown-kyoto), [Fushimi](https://www.insidekyoto.com/exploring-fushimi-kyotos-sake-district), (optional: [Gion](https://www.insidekyoto.com/gion)), [Arashiyama](https://www.insidekyoto.com/arashiyama), [Central Kyoto](https://www.insidekyoto.com/central-kyoto), [Kurama/Kibune](https://www.insidekyoto.com/kurama-and-kibune), [Northern Higashiyama](https://www.insidekyoto.com/northern-higashiyama).
* **Sights Visited:** [Kiyomizu-dera Temple](https://www.insidekyoto.com/kiyomizu-dera-temple), [Sannen and Ninen-zaka Area](https://www.insidekyoto.com/ninen-zaka-and-sannen-zaka-preserved-districts), [Chion-in Temple](https://www.insidekyoto.com/chion-in-temple), [Shoren-in Temple](https://www.insidekyoto.com/shoren-in-temple), [Downtown](https://www.insidekyoto.com/downtown-kyoto), [Nishiki Market](https://www.insidekyoto.com/nishiki-market-downtown-kyoto), [Fushimi-Inari-Taisha Shrine](https://www.insidekyoto.com/fushimi-inari-taisha-shrine), (optional: [Gion Shimbashi](https://www.insidekyoto.com/shimbashi-geisha-district-in-the-evening)); [Arashiyama](https://www.insidekyoto.com/arashiyama), [Kinkaku-ji Temple](https://www.insidekyoto.com/kinkaku-ji-temple-the-golden-pavilion) and [Daitoku-ji Temple](https://www.insidekyoto.com/daitoku-ji-temple), [Kurama-dera Temple](https://www.insidekyoto.com/kurama-dera-mountain-temple), [Ginkaku-ji Temple](https://www.insidekyoto.com/ginkaku-ji-temple-northern-higashiyama), [Honen-in Temple](https://www.insidekyoto.com/honen-in-temple-northern-higashiyama), [the Path of Philosophy](https://www.insidekyoto.com/the-path-of-philosophy-northern-higashiyama) and [Nanzen-ji Temple](https://www.insidekyoto.com/nanzen-ji-temple-northern-higashiyama).
* **Route Map:** Scroll down for a route map.
##
Check Hotel Availability
###
Destination
#### Check-in date
#### Check-out date
![Booking.com](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20width='150'%20height='0'%20viewBox='0%200%20150%200'%3E%3C/svg%3E)
### Day 1: [Southern Higashiyama](https://www.insidekyoto.com/southern-higashiyama), [Downtown](https://www.insidekyoto.com/downtown-kyoto) and [Fushimi-Inari](https://www.insidekyoto.com/fushimi-inari-taisha-shrine)
![Tree-top view of Kiyomizu-dera Temple, Kyoto, on a sunny day](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20width='800'%20height='534'%20viewBox='0%200%20800%20534'%3E%3C/svg%3E "Tree-top view of Kiyomizu-dera Temple, Kyoto, on a sunny day")Kiyomizu-dera Temple © [wkc1](https://www.flickr.com/)
**8:30am:** [Kiyomizu-dera Temple](https://www.insidekyoto.com/kiyomizu-dera-temple)
Take a bus or taxi to the Higashiyama-Gojo intersection. The closest bus stop is Gojozaka. Walk up Gojo-zaka, which is the main street that leads east, up the hill, to Kiyomizu-dera Temple. Most likely, you can just follow the people. Halfway up, you can bear right up Chawan-zaka, which gets you away from most of the cars and buses. Enter the temple and fully explore the temple. Don’t forget to visit the wonderful Tainai-Meguri.
![Japanese people and tourists walk along the Ninen-zaka area pedestrian street in Kyoto](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20width='750'%20height='600'%20viewBox='0%200%20750%20600'%3E%3C/svg%3E "Japanese people and tourists walk along the Ninen-zaka area pedestrian street in Kyoto")Ninen-zaka area © [sergejf](https://www.flickr.com/photos/sergejf/33188997353)
**9:30am:** [Sannen-zaka and Ninen-zaka](https://www.insidekyoto.com/ninen-zaka-and-sannen-zaka-preserved-districts), then [Maruyama-koen Park](https://www.insidekyoto.com/maruyama-koen-park)
After exploring Kiyomizu-dera, exit via the front of the temple and walk down Matsubara-dori Street (lined with shops) to reach the lovely preserved district of Sannen-zaka Hill. Continue down the street to where it flattens out and then go a bit further and take a right down into Ninen-zaka, another lovely preserved district. You will come to a larger cross street with cars. Go left down the hill then quickly turn right into [Nene-no-Michi, which is mostly free from cars](https://www.insidekyoto.com/nene-no-michi-lane). Follow this north, with one quick right-left jog to reach Maruyama-koen Park.
![View of the gatehouse at Chion-in temple in Kyoto](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20width='800'%20height='534'%20viewBox='0%200%20800%20534'%3E%3C/svg%3E "View of the gatehouse at Chion-in temple in Kyoto")Chion-in Temple gate © [ruthanddave](https://www.flickr.com/photos/ruthanddave/403755248)
**10:30am:** [Chion-in Temple](https://www.insidekyoto.com/chion-in-temple)
Exit the north side of Maruyama-koen and follow the street north to the impressive main gate of Chion-in Temple. Climb the steep steps and enter the wide courtyard of Chion-in. Enter the main hall and sit down to soak up the amazing atmosphere (just remember that this is a religious building). Return down the steps and continue north.
![Bright red and dark green foliage at the Shoren-in Temple garden, Kyoto](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20width='650'%20height='437'%20viewBox='0%200%20650%20437'%3E%3C/svg%3E "Bright red and dark green foliage at the Shoren-in Temple garden, Kyoto")
Shoren-in Temple garden – image © Damien Douxchamps
**11:00am:** [Shoren-in Temple](https://www.insidekyoto.com/shoren-in-temple)
You’ll soon pass the enormous camphor trees in front of Shoren-in Temple. Enter the temple and enjoy a nice cup of green tea and a sweet while looking out over the garden.
![View of hallway in Keage Station, Kyoto Municipal Subway Tozai Line](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20width='800'%20height='600'%20viewBox='0%200%20800%20600'%3E%3C/svg%3E "View of hallway in Keage Station, Kyoto Municipal Subway Tozai Line")
Tozai subway line station © [: : Ys [waiz] : :](https://flic.kr/p/ZT4kDo) / [CC BY-ND 2.0](https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-nd/2.0/)
**11:45:** Travel to [Downtown Kyoto](https://www.insidekyoto.com/downtown-kyoto)
Exit Shoren-in and walk north (downhill) to reach Sanjo-dori Street. Walk a short distance west (left) on Sanjo-dori to reach Higashiyama Station on [the Tozai subway line](https://www.insidekyoto.com/kyoto-subways). Take it two stops west and get off at Kyoto Shiyakushomae (downtown).
![Busy street scene in Downtown Kyoto, with people using a pedestrian crossing and lots of traffic](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20width='800'%20height='600'%20viewBox='0%200%20800%20600'%3E%3C/svg%3E "Busy street scene in Downtown Kyoto, with people using a pedestrian crossing and lots of traffic")Downtown Kyoto © [kahtava](https://www.flickr.com/photos/kahtava/120698326)
**Noon:** Lunch Downtown
There’s an almost endless selection of places to eat downtown. For some picks, check out our [Downtown Kyoto District](https://www.insidekyoto.com/downtown-kyoto) page.
![Customers look at a stall selling fresh vegetables in Nishiki Ichiba Market, Kyoto](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20width='800'%20height='531'%20viewBox='0%200%20800%20531'%3E%3C/svg%3E "Customers look at a stall selling fresh vegetables in Nishiki Ichiba Market, Kyoto")Nishiki Market © [stupid\_dream](https://www.flickr.com/photos/stupid_dream/9613995516)
**1:00pm:** Nishiki Market
After eating lunch, make your way to the east end of [Nishiki Market](https://www.insidekyoto.com/nishiki-market-downtown-kyoto) (where it joins the [Teramachi Shopping Arcade](https://www.insidekyoto.com/teramachi-shinkyogoku-shopping-arcades-kyoto)). It’s usually crowded here, even during the off-season. Hint: If you get tired of the crowds, you can bail by walking one block south and continuing along Shijo-dori Street.
![Customers pass cake counters in the Daimaru Department Store Food Floor, Kyoto](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20width='800'%20height='532'%20viewBox='0%200%20800%20532'%3E%3C/svg%3E "Customers pass cake counters in the Daimaru Department Store Food Floor, Kyoto")
Daimaru Department Store Food Floor, Kyoto – image © Chris Rowthorn
**2:00pm:** Daimaru Basement Food Floor
After you emerge from the west end of Nishiki, continue straight for a block and you’ll see [Daimaru Department Store](https://www.insidekyoto.com/daimaru-basement-food-floor-kyoto) on your left. Go down the stairs in front of the entrance and enter the food floor. Explore the food floor and be sure to check out the Japanese sweets and tea section.
![Two trains pass each other on the Keihan Line in Kyoto](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20width='800'%20height='534'%20viewBox='0%200%20800%20534'%3E%3C/svg%3E "Two trains pass each other on the Keihan Line in Kyoto") Keihan Line trains © [115599971@N02](https://www.flickr.com/photos/115599971@N02/32034383373)
**2:30pm:** Take the Keihan Line to Fushimi-Inari Station
Exit Daimaru via the main entrance onto Shijo-dori Street. Walk east on Shijo-dori, [across the Kamo-gawa River](https://www.insidekyoto.com/kamo-gawa-riverside), and walk downstairs into Keihan Gion-Shijo Station. [Take the Keihan Line](https://www.insidekyoto.com/kyoto-trains) south to Fushimi-Inari Station (all trains EXCEPT the limited express stop at Fushimi-Inari).
![The distinctive bright red posts of the Fushimi-Inari-Taisha Shrine, Kyoto](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20width='800'%20height='533'%20viewBox='0%200%20800%20533'%3E%3C/svg%3E "The distinctive bright red posts of the Fushimi-Inari-Taisha Shrine, Kyoto")Fushimi-Inari-Taisha Shrine © [themonnie](https://www.flickr.com/photos/themonnie/3636862418)
**3:00pm:** [Fushimi-Inari-Taisha Shrine](https://www.insidekyoto.com/fushimi-inari-taisha-shrine)
The way is clearly marked from the station. Walk out of the station, take a left and walk up the hill, crossing the JR tracks. After crossing one relatively major street, you will see the first torii (gate) of the shrine. Walk up through this and you’ll shortly get to the main precinct of the shrine. Our [Fushimi-Inari Hike](https://www.insidekyoto.com/fushimi-inari-hike-kyoto) gives a detailed route description. We don’t suggest doing the whole route on this day, but going up as far as Shin-ike Pond is a good idea.
**Late Afternoon:** Return to hotel for rest
By this point, you’ll definitely be in need of a rest. Take [either the Keihan Line or the JR Line](https://www.insidekyoto.com/kyoto-trains) back from Fushimi-Inari to [central Kyoto](https://www.insidekyoto.com/central-kyoto), depending upon where in town you are staying.
![Atmospheric lights reflected in the Kyoto Kamo-gawa River at night](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20width='800'%20height='450'%20viewBox='0%200%20800%20450'%3E%3C/svg%3E "Atmospheric lights reflected in the Kyoto Kamo-gawa River at night")Kyoto Kamo-gawa River in the evening © [zoonyzoozoodazoo](https://www.flickr.com/)
**7:00pm:** Dinner Downtown
Downtown Kyoto has the best selection of restaurants in town. For some picks, check out our [Downtown Kyoto District](https://www.insidekyoto.com/downtown-kyoto) page.
![Atmospheric lights reflected in the Gion Shimbashi in the evening, Kyoto](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20width='800'%20height='534'%20viewBox='0%200%20800%20534'%3E%3C/svg%3E "Atmospheric lights reflected in the Gion Shimbashi in the evening, Kyoto")Gion Shimbashi in the evening © [kentwang](https://www.flickr.com/)
**After Dinner:** [Gion Shimbashi](https://www.insidekyoto.com/shimbashi-geisha-district-in-the-evening)
After dinner, if you still have energy, a [walk through Gion](https://www.insidekyoto.com/walking-in-gion) is a great idea. Start from Shijo-Ohashi Bridge (the big bridge on Shijo-dori [over the Kamo-gawa River](https://www.insidekyoto.com/kamo-gawa-riverside)). At the east end of the bridge, cross Kawabata-dori and [walk north on the east side of Kawabata](https://www.insidekyoto.com/kyotos-most-romantic-walks) for about 100 meters. You will soon see a tree-lined pedestrian lane. This is the end of Shirakawa-suji. Follow it east and you’ll get to [the Gion-Shimbashi District](https://www.insidekyoto.com/shimbashi-geisha-district-in-the-evening) (also known as Shirakawa), which is the most beautiful street in Kyoto. In busy seasons, it will be crowded with people. After checking it out, make your way through the alleys south to Shijo-dori and walk east to Shijo and cross over into Hanami-koji, another picturesque lane. [This is geisha territory](https://www.insidekyoto.com/hanamikoji-geisha-district-in-the-evening), so keep your eyes peeled.
### Day 2: Arashiyama, Kinkaku-ji and Daitoku-ji
**8:30am:** Head west to Arashiyama by taxi, train or bus
Your choice of transport out to Arashiyama will depend on where you are staying and your willingness to take taxis. See our [Arashiyama District](https://www.insidekyoto.com/arashiyama) page for transport information to Arashiyama (scroll to near the end of the page).
![A wide angle view of the garden and pond at Tenryu-ji Temple, Kyoto, will hills in the background](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20width='800'%20height='330'%20viewBox='0%200%20800%20330'%3E%3C/svg%3E "A wide angle view of the garden and pond at Tenryu-ji Temple, Kyoto, will hills in the background")
Tenryu-ji Temple pond in spring © [neekohfi](https://www.flickr.com/photos/neekohfi/4517773734)
**9:00am:** [Tenryu-ji Temple](https://www.insidekyoto.com/tenryu-ji-temple-arashiyama)
First, explore the superb Tenryu-ji Temple, being sure to check out both the inside of the main hall and the expansive stroll garden.
![View of the path through the Arashiyama Bamboo Grove, Kyoto](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20width='800'%20height='534'%20viewBox='0%200%20800%20534'%3E%3C/svg%3E "View of the path through the Arashiyama Bamboo Grove, Kyoto")Arashiyama Bamboo Grove © [norwegian-cactus](https://www.flickr.com/photos/norwegian-cactus/36804336352)
**10:00am:** [Arashiyama Bamboo Grove](https://www.insidekyoto.com/arashiyama-bamboo-grove)
Leave Tenryu-ji by the north gate (accessible through the garden), and take a left, which will bring you into the famed Arashiyama Bamboo Grove. Take your time savoring the magical atmosphere here, while working slowly uphill.
![On the grounds of Okochi Sanso, a sprawling and stunning mountain villa built by a Japanese silent movie actor, Okochi Denjiro, Kyoto](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20width='800'%20height='600'%20viewBox='0%200%20800%20600'%3E%3C/svg%3E "On the grounds of Okochi Sanso, a sprawling and stunning mountain villa built by a Japanese silent movie actor, Okochi Denjiro, Kyoto")Okochi-Sanso Villa © [hslo](https://www.flickr.com/photos/hslo/4668931631)
**10:30am:** [Okochi-Sanso Villa](https://www.insidekyoto.com/okochi-sanso-villa-arashiyama)
At the top of the hill in the Bamboo Grove, you will see a small hut and a road leading up the hill into the forest. This is the entrance to the superb Okochi-Sanso Villa, the former home of a wealthy Japanese actor. Enter the villa and take your time exploring the garden. Be sure to hold onto the ticket they give you, because it will get you a nice cup of green tea and a sweet after the garden tour.
**11:30am:** Lunch in [Arashiyama](https://www.insidekyoto.com/arashiyama)
Leave Okochi-Sanso Villa and return to central Arashiyama. Eat a simple lunch of noodles or rice in one of the shokudo (noodle and rice restaurants) on the main strip. For some picks, see our Arashiyama District page.
**12:30pm:** Taxi to Kinkaku-ji Temple
There really isn’t a good way by public transport, so from Arashiyama, [take a taxi](https://www.insidekyoto.com/kyoto-taxis) over to Kinkaku-ji Temple, the famed “Golden Pavilion” in Northwest Kyoto. A taxi from Arashiyama to Kinkaku-ji will cost around ¥2000.
![The famous Golden Pavilion reflected in the way at Kyoto Kinkaku-Ji Temple](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20width='800'%20height='600'%20viewBox='0%200%20800%20600'%3E%3C/svg%3E "The famous Golden Pavilion reflected in the way at Kyoto Kinkaku-Ji Temple")Kinkaku-ji Temple © [neepster](https://www.flickr.com/photos/neepster/289370830)
**1:00pm:** Kinkaku-ji Temple
Take your time strolling through [the grounds of Kinkaku-ji](https://www.insidekyoto.com/kinkaku-ji-temple-the-golden-pavilion). Yes, it will most likely be crowded at this time, but it almost always is.
**2:00pm:** Taxi to Daitoku-ji Temple
The next stop is the magical Zen world of Daitoku-ji Temple. [The easiest and fastest way to get from Kinaku-ji to Daitoku-ji is a taxi](https://www.insidekyoto.com/kyoto-taxis), which will cost around ¥1000. If you prefer public transport, you can [go from Kinkaku-ji to Daitoku-ji by bus](https://www.insidekyoto.com/kyoto-buses) (No 204/205). Or, you can walk there in about 20 minutes. Note that if you’re starting to feel templed out by this point, consider cutting out Daitoku-ji and just head back to [downtown Kyoto](https://www.insidekyoto.com/downtown-kyoto). Buses run for the Kinkakuji-michi stop to downtown and Kyoto Station.
![The zen temple of Zuiho-in, in the Daitoku-ji area, Kyoto](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20width='650'%20height='435'%20viewBox='0%200%20650%20435'%3E%3C/svg%3E "The zen temple of Zuiho-in, in the Daitoku-ji area, Kyoto")
Zuiho-in Subtemple at Daitoku-ji – image © Damien Douxchamps
**2:30pm:** Daitoku-ji Temple
Explore the Zen world of Daitoku-ji Temple. Consider entering one of the [subtemples like Koto-in](https://www.insidekyoto.com/koto-subtemple). For full details on Daitoku-ji, [see our Exploring Daitoku-ji Temple page](https://www.insidekyoto.com/exploring-daitoku-ji).
**3:00pm:** Karasuma Subway Line to Shijo Station
From Daitoku-ji, it’s about a 20-minute walk east on Kitaoji-dori Street to reach Kitaoji Station on the [Karasuma Subway Line](https://www.insidekyoto.com/kyoto-subways). This subway will get you to Shijo Station or to Kyoto Station in a few minutes.
**Late Afternoon:** Rest at your hotel
By this point, you’ll certainly be in need of some down time.
![Night-time view of a street in Downtown Kyoto at night](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20width='800'%20height='481'%20viewBox='0%200%20800%20481'%3E%3C/svg%3E "Night-time view of a street in Downtown Kyoto at night")Downtown Kyoto in the evening © [cezzie901](https://www.flickr.com/photos/cezzie901/15531022474/)
**6:30pm:** Dinner Downtown
Downtown Kyoto has the best selection of restaurants in town. For some picks, check out our [Downtown Kyoto District](https://www.insidekyoto.com/downtown-kyoto) page.
![Atmospheric illuminated traditional Pontocho Alley at night](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20width='800'%20height='531'%20viewBox='0%200%20800%20531'%3E%3C/svg%3E "Atmospheric illuminated traditional Pontocho Alley at night")
Pontocho Alley in the evening © [ajari](https://www.flickr.com/photos/ajari/3263449106)
**8:00pm:** Stroll Downtown
After dinner, consider taking an evening stroll along [Pontocho Alley](https://www.insidekyoto.com/pontocho-alley-kyoto) or down the lovely stretch of Kiyamachi-dori south of Shijo-dori.

### Day 3: Kurama/Kibune and Northern Higashiyama
![Steps leading up to Kurama-dera Temple, Kyoto, with traditional red lamps on either side](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20width='800'%20height='534'%20viewBox='0%200%20800%20534'%3E%3C/svg%3E "Steps leading up to Kurama-dera Temple, Kyoto, with traditional red lamps on either side")Steps up to Kurama-dera Temple © [143466180@N07](https://www.flickr.com/)
**Morning:** [Kurama/Kibune](https://www.insidekyoto.com/kurama-and-kibune)
In the morning, take a half-day trip up to the village of Kurama and do the hike over the mountain via the temple to the quaint village of Kibune. We give full transport directions and hiking directions on our [Kurama to Kibune Hike](https://www.insidekyoto.com/kurama-to-kibune-hike) page.

Kyoto’s key areas for
places to stay[More Info](https://www.insidekyoto.com/where-to-stay-in-kyoto?utm_source=grid&utm_medium=link&utm_id=IK-Practical-Grid-03)
**Noon or 1:00pm:** Lunch back in Kyoto
After doing the Kurama to Kibune hike, take the Eizan train back to Demachiyanagi and eat lunch somewhere near the station. There are plenty of restaurants in the Hyakumamben area, which is the intersection of Imadegawa and Higashioji, about 10 minutes’ walk east of Demachiyanagi Station. For some restaurant recommendations, see our [Northern Higashiyama District](https://www.insidekyoto.com/northern-higashiyama) page.
![An autumnal view of Ginkakuji, the Temple of the Silver Pavilion, surrounded by fall foliage](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20width='800'%20height='534'%20viewBox='0%200%20800%20534'%3E%3C/svg%3E "An autumnal view of Ginkakuji, the Temple of the Silver Pavilion, surrounded by fall foliage")Ginkaku-ji Temple © [jmhullot](https://www.flickr.com/photos/jmhullot/16293739330)
**2:00pm:** Ginkaku-ji Temple
After eating lunch, head east along Imadegawa-dori toward the Higashiyama mountains. It will take about 20 minutes to [walk up to Ginkaku-ji Temple](https://www.insidekyoto.com/ginkaku-ji-temple-northern-higashiyama), which is at the base of the mountains. A taxi will get you there in a few minutes for around ¥700 from Hyakumamben. Enter Ginkaku-ji Temple and explore.
![Kyoto’s Path of Philosophy shown under spring cherry blossoms](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20width='800'%20height='600'%20viewBox='0%200%20800%20600'%3E%3C/svg%3E "Kyoto’s Path of Philosophy shown under spring cherry blossoms")
Path of Philosophy – Kyoto © [andersondotcom](https://www.flickr.com/photos/andersondotcom/484667324)
**3:00pm:** Path of Philosophy
After exploring Ginkaku-ji Temple, walk about 200 meters down the hill and walk south along [the famed Path of Philosophy (Tetsugaku-no-Michi)](https://www.insidekyoto.com/the-path-of-philosophy-northern-higashiyama). It’s the canal that has a pedestrian path running alongside it. If you want, you can take a short detour up the hill to visit the lovely [Honen-in Temple](https://www.insidekyoto.com/honen-in-temple-northern-higashiyama).
![Long view of the Nanzen-ji Temple San-Mon Gate, Kyoto](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20width='800'%20height='600'%20viewBox='0%200%20800%20600'%3E%3C/svg%3E "Long view of the Nanzen-ji Temple San-Mon Gate, Kyoto")
Nanzen-ji Temple San-Mon Gate © [mshades](https://www.flickr.com/photos/mshades/517032497)
**4:00pm:** Nanzen-ji Temple
Continue walking south along the Path of Philosophy to the southern end, and then work your way further south to [Nanzen-ji Temple](https://www.insidekyoto.com/nanzen-ji-temple-northern-higashiyama) (you’ll pass a high school en route). Even if you’re there after they close the inner garden, you can explore the grounds any time you want (the outer grounds are open 24 hours). After exploring the temple, you can walk a little south and west to reach Keage Station on [the Tozai subway line](https://www.insidekyoto.com/kyoto-subways). This will take you [downtown](https://www.insidekyoto.com/downtown-kyoto) for dinner or for a rest at your hotel. Alternatively, [there’s a taxi rank](https://www.insidekyoto.com/kyoto-taxis) near the front gate of Nanzen-ji.
It’s important to think about your hotel location for making the most of your time in Kyoto. See [Where To Stay In Kyoto](https://www.insidekyoto.com/where-to-stay-in-kyoto) for a rundown of the most convenient Kyoto districts for sightseeing.
### Route Map
The Kyoto 3-Day Itinerary map shows the location of each of the places mentioned. You can view a [full screen version](https://drive.google.com/open?id=1BEpYF6HBxzBT_QcxPn297aMf61GBMbg&usp=sharing) too.
### Kyoto Vacation Checklist
* [ESSENTIALS](https://www.insidekyoto.com/first-time-in-kyoto?utm_source=checkbox&utm_medium=link&utm_campaign=IK-Checklist-01) – For all the essentials in a brief overview, see my [First Time In Kyoto](https://www.insidekyoto.com/first-time-in-kyoto?utm_source=checkbox&utm_medium=link&utm_campaign=IK-Checklist-02) guide
* [HOTELS](https://www.booking.com/city/jp/kyoto.html?aid=965263;label=IK-Checklist-03) – Check Kyoto accommodation availability on [Booking.com](https://www.booking.com/city/jp/kyoto.html?aid=965263;label=IK-Checklist-04) and [Agoda.com](https://www.agoda.com/partners/partnersearch.aspx?pcs=1&cid=1592349&city=1784&tag=IK-Checklist-05) - often you can book with no upfront payment and free cancellation
* [BULLET TRAIN](https://affiliate.klook.com/redirect?aid=1591&aff_adid=826984&k_site=https://www.klook.com/en-US/activity/81022-japan-rails/) – You can buy [shinkansen (bullet train) tickets online](https://affiliate.klook.com/redirect?aid=1591&aff_adid=826984&k_site=https://www.klook.com/en-US/activity/81022-japan-rails/) from Klook - popular routes include [Tokyo to Kyoto](https://affiliate.klook.com/redirect?aid=1591&aff_adid=826982&k_site=https://www.klook.com/rails-32/1012-japan/route-1305/tokyo-to-kyoto-trains/), [Kyoto to Osaka](https://affiliate.klook.com/redirect?aid=1591&aff_adid=826982&k_site=https://www.klook.com/rails-32/1012-japan/route-1474/kyoto-to-osaka-trains/) and [Kyoto to Tokyo](https://affiliate.klook.com/redirect?aid=1591&aff_adid=826982&k_site=https://www.klook.com/rails-32/1012-japan/route-1307/kyoto-to-tokyo-trains/)
* [WHERE TO STAY](https://www.insidekyoto.com/where-to-stay-in-kyoto?utm_source=checkbox&utm_medium=link&utm_campaign=IK-Checklist-06) – Need tips on where to stay? See my one page guide [Where To Stay In Kyoto](https://www.insidekyoto.com/where-to-stay-in-kyoto?utm_source=checkbox&utm_medium=link&utm_campaign=IK-Checklist-07)
* [PACKING LIST](https://www.insidekyoto.com/packing-list-for-japan?utm_source=checkbox&utm_medium=link&utm_campaign=IK-Checklist-08) – See my comprehensive [Packing List For Japan](https://www.insidekyoto.com/packing-list-for-japan?utm_source=checkbox&utm_medium=link&utm_campaign=IK-Checklist-09)
* [SIM CARDS](https://affiliate.klook.com/redirect?aid=2652&aff_adid=877200&k_site=https://www.klook.com/search/result/?query=japan%20esim) – You can buy an [eSim to activate in Japan](https://affiliate.klook.com/redirect?aid=1591&aff_adid=877279&k_site=https://www.klook.com/search/result/?query=japan%20esim) or buy a data-only SIM card online for collection when you arrive at [Tokyo's Narita or Haneda Airports](<https://www.getyourguide.com/s/?q=tokyo sim&searchSource=8&partner_id=LAMDN2W&utm_medium=online_publisher&placement=content-middle&cmp=IK-Checklist-10>) or [Kansai International Airport](<https://www.getyourguide.com/s/?q=kansai sim&searchSource=8&partner_id=LAMDN2W&utm_medium=online_publisher&placement=content-middle&cmp=IK-Checklist-11>). You can also rent an [unlimited data pocket wifi router](<https://www.getyourguide.com/s/?q=japan wifi&searchSource=7&partner_id=LAMDN2W&utm_medium=online_publisher&placement=content-middle&cmp=IK-Checklist-12>)
* [FLIGHTS](https://www.skyscanner.net/g/referrals/v1/flights/cheap-flights-to?adId=1100662&campaignId=13416&destination=tyoa&mediaPartnerId=1924024) – Compare Japan flight prices and timings to [find the best deals](https://www.skyscanner.net/g/referrals/v1/flights/cheap-flights-to?adId=1100662&campaignId=13416&destination=tyoa&mediaPartnerId=1924024)
* [JAPAN RAIL PASS](https://affiliate.klook.com/redirect?aid=1591&aff_adid=616549&k_site=https://www.klook.com/activity/1420-7-day-whole-japan-rail-pass-jr-pass/) – If you're making frequent train journeys during your visit, you might save money with [Japan Rail Pass](https://affiliate.klook.com/redirect?aid=1591&aff_adid=616549&k_site=https://www.klook.com/activity/1420-7-day-whole-japan-rail-pass-jr-pass/) – see [if it's worth it](https://www.insidekyoto.com/japan-rail-pass-is-it-worth-it?utm_source=checkbox&utm_medium=link&utm_campaign=IK-Checklist-13) for you
* [TRAVEL INSURANCE](https://www.tkqlhce.com/click-7919142-15371527) – [World Nomads](https://www.tkqlhce.com/click-7647395-15371527) offers simple and flexible travel insurance. Buy at home or while traveling and claim online from anywhere in the world
* [WANT HELP?](https://www.insidekyoto.com/japan-travel-consulting?utm_source=checkbox&utm_medium=link&utm_campaign=IK-Checklist-14) – Do you want help planning your trip? Chris Rowthorn and his team of Japan experts at [Japan Travel Consulting](https://www.insidekyoto.com/japan-travel-consulting?utm_source=checkbox&utm_medium=link&utm_campaign=IK-Checklist-15) can help

### About InsideKyoto.com and Chris Rowthorn
* Chris Rowthorn is the co-founder of [InsideKyoto.com](https://www.insidekyoto.com) and has an unparalleled travel knowledge of Kyoto spanning three decades. Chris has written or commissioned and edited every page on the Inside Kyoto website.
* A former author of **Lonely Planet Kyoto** and **Lonely Planet Japan** guidebooks, Chris is also the director of the Kyoto walking tour company [Chris Rowthorn Tours](http://www.chrisrowthorn.com), and [Japan Travel Consulting](https://insideosaka.com/japan-travel-consulting/), where you can get bespoke travel advice for your Japan trip.
* [InsideKyoto.com](https://www.insidekyoto.com) has been cited by the **[New York Times](https://www.nytimes.com/2013/03/17/travel/booking-hotels-in-asia-the-getaway.html)** and **[The Times of London](https://www.thetimes.com/article/926c41d6-bb7d-11e5-8ae7-f2021079f4dd)**, among many other publications and websites.
